text: Ferroelectric (FE) semiconductors, such as BaTiO₃, have a remnant polarization that can aid in the separation of photo-generated charge carriers. Here we report for the first time that BaTiO₃ nanocrystals have been made to have enhanced photocatalytic H₂ production and photoelectrochemical water oxidation due to the ferroelectric effect. Tetragonal-phase nanocrystals were hydrothermally synthesized from TiO₂ and barium hydroxide in 63% yield. Films of the nanocrystals were grown on tantalum and under UV light (60 mW cm⁻²) at 1.23 V vs. RHE (vs. the reversible hydrogen electrode) a photocurrent of 0.141 mA cm⁻² was measured. Following electric polarization at 52.8 kV cm⁻¹ along the substrate normal, the photocurrent was found to increase by a factor of 2 by applying a negative field and decrease by a factor of 3.5 by applying a positive field. The onset potential of the photocurrent was shifted by −0.15 V for the negative field or by +0.09 V for the positive field and the surface photovoltage response was altered following polarization. Similarly, the photocatalytic production of H₂ by Pt/BaTiO₃ and the selectivity of silver photodeposition on the (001) facets were both enhanced by a factor of 1.5 following polarization. The ferroelectric effects on the photoactivity of BaTiO₃ were fully reversible and could be diminished by heating the samples above the Curie temperature. Thus, the effects on the space charge layer caused by the dipole field have been made use of in order to realize an enhancement of the solar to fuel conversion efficiency.
explanation: The text is highly data-driven with specific experimental values and a coherent scientific narrative, which is more typical of human-authored material; while advanced AI could produce similar text, the precise results and phrasing suggest a likely human origin.
